3rd-strata/hytropo.html.title: Hytropo
3rd-strata/hytropo.html.description: The realm is named Hytrop(i/a/o). (Hi-trope-(ee/ah/oh))Each denizen calls it either Hytropi, Hytropa, or Hytropo; based upon the gender they identify with. -i for non-binary or fluid, -a for feminine, and -o for masculine.-e (ey) is another suffix, used for neutral gender. -u (oo) is another suffix used for unknown genders or to be formal. Attaching a -y to a suffix turns it plural.Hytrope and Hytropu are uncommon forms of the realm’s name, but possible to encounter.Within the language of the denizens of the realm, all nouns used these suffixes. To use a car as an example, if no one was driving it, the word would end in -e, as it is a simple object without a will or soul, but if a man was driving it, it would end in -o and a woman driving it, -a.Names utilize this method as well, however, it is based on the person who owns the name to choose a suffix, unlike with objects.Terrain; Cloudpost, Glimmerpost, Trenchpost L^2 Planar Nexus 4 / Lsn Sliver Hive s Raffine's Tower n Contaminated Landscape 2n Glimmervoid n^1 Mana Confluence n^2 Echoes of Eternity n^3 Hive; Thrumming Hivepool triangular(n) Riptide Island sqrt(n) Crawlspace s Non-Legendary Slivers n Animals; Gremlins Fungi Pests Worms Chromium Eldrazi Sapients; Splinter Kin - Faerie, Insect, Germ Dreamer

aetherborn.html.title: Aetherborn
aetherborn.html.description:
Aetherborn also known as 'Dreamers' amongst the realmic denizens, awaken at the edge of a realm. As if they woke up from a pleasant dream on the shores of a beach.

Yahenni, Undying Partisan by Lius Lasahido
They are all considered to be gifted, capable of extending their lives by draining the souls of others or consuming the mana crystals, etherium, and moxite. Though, their deaths come inexorably nonetheless.They are capable of travelling between the realms by 'jumping off the edge' of a realm. Though a few are capable of doing so from any point on a realm, in a manner similar to planeswalking. Those who jump off the edge of a realm will dissipate and then reform on the shore of another realm, like the day they were 'born', with memories of the prior realm being akin to a vivid dream. This does not reset their lifespans.Due to this power, they are the only group of peoples to have a multi-realmic society.Those who integrate with the denizens of a particular realm may tell stories or tales of the other realms, pure fanciful myths to all but a few.In particular, there is a militaristic faction of their people who wish to monopolize the resources of every realm, and that they should conquer them, eradicating the slivers at their centers. They have gained support with older Aetherborn, but the newer ones, the ones who have yet to come close to their mortality, do not have the same opinion. Some claim this thinking to be dangerous while others claim that this is their right given their ability to traverse the space between.
From the scraps of Remnants, they have reverse engineered a sort of power armor, fueled by Moxite and themselves, granting them extremely enhanced physical abilities.

Ironclad Revolutionary by Eric Deschamps
They have independently invented their own weapons out of Etherium, mana guns. The Lance, a handheld weapon, and the rifle, a two handed heaver variant.
Lances condense the ambient mana into a bolt and acts as a sort of railgun for the bolt. It takes a number of seconds to charge a single shot equal to the number of the realm's colors and does not work on Komas. Effectively, it deals 1 damage to target creature.

Kellan, the Kid by Magali Villeneuve
Rifles use mana crystals to overcharge the power into a potent spell, with a cooldown of triangular(X) * .5 seconds (.5 seconds for a crystal from Komas, 10.5 seconds for a crystal from Corona). Each mana crystal can sustain sixty five shots. X is equal to the number of colors of the mana crystal plus 1.

Taii Wakeen, Perfect Shot by David Auden Nash

echo.html.title: Echo
echo.html.description: The gestalt souls of the dead that have formed physical bodies. Their natures vary significantly between the realms, both in personality and number.The one thing that is consistent, relatively, is their appearance. They take the shape of a Fomori with a featureless face, their shadows are like a Nyxborn's, their hair flames composed of mana (the colors of the realm), and their flesh a smooth black stone with veins of Etherium within.

engine.html.title: Engine
engine.html.description:
The Planar Portal that enables travel to the multiverse and [other realms](War Web).
The Eternity Column that enables travel to the exoverse and the other realms (if you know the location of the others).
It coordinates the leylines from both realities towards the center, funnels souls into itself, it facilitates the stabilization of the chaos wall into something substantially real, the expansion of the realms, and the drive of the sliver hives to protect it.The engine itself is often underground, a massive sphere of liquid silver in another spherical cave. What little space between the engine and the cave is a torrent of energy, mana, and dust. A maelstrom of wind cycling the energy around the entire space and into the liquid metal. The whispers of the dead can be sometimes heard in the wind, the windswept cave walls are pockmarked with holes, that lead to the sliver hive proper, the ambient mana flowing into the space plentiful enough to generate not only light but also heat.
In every realm, save Corona, a single drop of silvery metal with fall from the engine every fifty five minutes. On the floor of this cave, it will congeal and coalesce into a Thrum, which is forcefully integrated into the realm's hive by the engine above.In Corona, the engine has fractured and split into many pieces, each contributes the the chaotic expansion of the realm and all but one of these engines spawns a Thrum at different rates. The central engine, the one 'core' of Corona does not spawn any.

kin.html.title: Kin
kin.html.description:
The Kin are chimeric humanoids that take on the forms of specific beings that have survived upon the realms. As the realms expanded, they too became larger, until they have all become the size of a human, on average.Their cousins, who did not gain sapience, are smaller, cat sized at their largest, and in many cases, significantly smaller than that. The ones that did gain sapience have taken a more humanoid, or standing upright, appearance if their animalistic form does not already have one.Colorless - Pest Salamander/Frog-like creatures, often distinguished by being colorful, voracious, and being covered in spines, teeth, and claws. They have colored patterns on their bodies, one of the colors of which are associated with the core color of the realm. An example, for a realm where black is the core color of mana, they could have a purple or black color to their pattern, on top of potentially other random colors. Colorless is grey and WUBRG is a metallic gold. Their patterns often have three or four colors, but only one of which is guaranteed to be associated with the realm's core.

Blech, Loafing Pest by Ilse Gort
White - Faerie Diminutive humanoids with legs that end in small points, they are fond of tricks, mischief, thievery, jokes, and being helpful. Their wings are ten fabric-like ribbons of light that extend from their backs. They have small, smooth, curved horns. They have eight eyes, similar to a spider. Their skin is striped and shiny, similar to a wasp, though rather than yellow, they have human skin tones. Finally their hair is a metallic color, depending on the core color of the realm. Gold for white, Silver for blue, Lead for black, Iron for red, and Copper for green, etherium and moxite for colorless and WUBRG.
Blue - Insect Six limbed, four arms and two legs for sapients, have large compound eyes across the sides of their heads. They have hair along their bodies, most notably as a sort of mane. They are a sort of species of ant-like beetle.

Chitin Gravestalker by Slawomir Maniak
Red - Gremlin They are six-limbed, vaguely anteater- or aardvark-like creatures that feed on aether, dismantling artifacts to get it. Their saliva is corrosive, allowing them to breach through metal hulls to reach their food. Their young are called grubs.

Gimbal, Gremlin Prodigy by Fajareka Setiawan
Black - Germ They are, generally speaking, microscopic organisms. However, they are responsible for the chimeric nature of the sapients, be it they are a part of it or not. The sapients are large colonial organisms of germs, looking like they are made of goo with the 'glow' color of the realm's core color. Yellow for White, Light Blue for Blue, Purple for Black, Bright Red for Red, Pale Green for Green, and Rainbow/Iridescent/Oilslick for WUBRG.

Irregular Cohort by Steve Argyle
Green - [Fungi](https://mtg.wiki/page/Varmint](https://mtg.wiki/page/Fungus) As the dominant plant-life of the realms, the non-sapients are extremely diverse. For the sapient varieties, their limbs are vine or root like, their heads look like some sort of fruiting body, they often have growths of fur-like moss on their bodies and limbs, and sometimes their bodies have slimy sacks within, akin to vital organs for a human. They feast on death and decay primarily.
Mycoloth by Raymond Swanland

remnant.html.title: Remnant
remnant.html.description:
Machines left by the Fomori, they have built up their own numbers, as time has progressed.
One modification they typically give to themselves is an eternity drive, which they mine Moxite to fuel.

slivers.html.title: Slivers
slivers.html.description:
Slivers have three methods of reproduction, one from the multiverse, one from the exoverse, and one unique to these [pocket realms](War Web).
The first, unique to these spaces, is autogenesis. The liquid metal pool in the heart of every hive that surrounds the Engine, periodically spawns thrum. The number spawned has increased over the course of centuries and millennia, initially having been one at 'midnight'.

Thrumming Hivepool by Rob Rey
The second, the multiversal method, is parthenogenesis. Slivers are capable of laying eggs via asexual reproduction. This can only be done while the individual's cortisol levels are low to non-existent, otherwise known as the individual feeling safe and secure. There are, of course, exceptions or modifications to this if the hiveform is specialized for reproduction in some capacity. Three notable examples being Sliver Queen, The First Sliver, and Hatchery Sliver. The queen can simply do this regardless of state and hatchery overdrives this process.

Hatchery Sliver by Svetlin VelinovThe third, the exoversal method, is oviparity. Specifically, utilizing externally fertilized parasitic ovipositors. Basically, xenomorphing people. Stabbing them with a specific tendrils that can extend from their beak-like 'faces' and implanting an embryo. It uses the genetics of the host to determine the color alignment and future adaptation (if any), technically the external fertilization is this process of utilizing the host's dna. If you are, at the time of being implanted, classified as a blue black being, then the sliver who emerges would either be colorless or blue black. The host will be unconscious for a day, due to adjacently injected toxins that quells the immune system and causes the host to believe they simply fell asleep rather than having been injected with an embryo, then it will develop over the course of ten days in the host, then spend five days after violently bursting from the host's torso to mature fully into a Thrum or fully adapted Sliver.

Syphon Sliver by Johann Bodin

splinter.html.title: Splinter
splinter.html.description:
Splinters are odd humanoids, highly social, yet oddly solitary. They are descendants of Slivers that 'evolved individualism' as best they can figure, around the time the Fomori disappeared. Overtime, they have lost a majority of their 'sliveriness', they barely have a hivemind, they are barely shapeshifters, and they are humanoid in form. They often regard the sliver hive in a similar manner to a human and a chimpanzee, not the same and not the same level of sentience/sapience generally, yet something that can definitely be dangerous if provoked.
On the hivemind point, they have a thought-weft. They are able to telepathically communicate with one another and share emotions, but not share their thoughts, memories, or experiences. It also lends them a general sense of the direction and distance of the nearest sliver, which being no longer in the hive, they avoid. As well as a far more accurate sense of the direction and distance of other splinters.On the humanoid point, beyond that, they look off. Their bodies are covered in chitinous plates (grey and marbled by the realm's colors), their eyes are more akin to gemstones that are the same color of the realm's colors mixed together, they have "hair" that is more like the squirming tentacles of a jellyfish or polyp than true hair, and they have cloaks of leathery flesh that is oddly similar to tanned human skin when at rest. They do not have ears or mouths that are visible, rather they seem to have plates around such structures, either protecting those structures or giving the illusion that such structures exist. Their humanoid appearance is assumed to be a subconscious adaptation to put the other sapients at ease, the cloak of flesh and their mouth/ear guards to mimic clothes. Compared to normal slivers, these adaptations were made over the course of millennia rather than moments. As a species, they have witnessed the other races arise and have cemented themselves as an enigmatic, yet benevolent, species within the collective racial memories of the other races.On the shapeshifter point, what little they can shapeshift depends drastically upon the realm they inhabit, though one trait they all share is the ability to change the texture and coloration of the outside of their fleshy cloaks much like an octopus can change their skin's texture and color.

war-web.html.title: War Web
war-web.html.description:
During the Fomori-Eldrazi War, the Fomori of the Multiverse travelled across the chaos wall frequently. To facilitate the supply lines for this war, they built staging grounds into the chaos wall, spaces between the finite infinity between the multiverse and the edge.
These pockets stabilized the chaos, concentrated it into mana, and then concentrated it into matter. A self perpetuating Engine of expansion that served as staging grounds, relay stations, and recharging stations. Each one aligned with different colors of mana to serve as perfect 'batteries' for different machines and purposes.
The Fomori had many war machines, but with the war against the Eldrazi and Ixalan both going simultaneously, they implemented an unusual force to safeguard these locations from the near constant invasions of the Eldrazi. They used a peculiar organism they found within both the edge and the multiverse, Slivers.
Making artifacts to control their hiveminds, they were able to have the Slivers reliably safeguard the planar portals and eternity columns that were built into the center of these pockets, allowing themselves, their ships, and their war machines to pass through uncontested, while aggressively fighting and adapting to the Eldrazi menace. A strategy not unlike the methods employed by Volrath to guard the Legacy, but on a more grand scale.The Fomori did not come into these pockets entirely devoid of other forms of life. Bringing in the slivers and coming in themselves, they had various hitchhikers, ancestors of the flora and fauna that can be found in the pockets today. Though, as they were not intentionally seeded with life nor designed to host life other than Slivers, major forms of life are few and not particularly diverse.
Since the Fomori disappeared, the pockets expanded, life continued to adapt, and the concentrations of mana warped the landscape. As they expanded, they weakened the wall in specific nebulous points, which has facilitated the crossing between the two occasionally. Such as Tezzeret entering into the Edge.
Various unintended side effects of these pockets have become prominent over the millennia, leylines from the Edge slowly grows Moxite along them, while leylines from the Multiverse slowly grows Etherium along them. They act as a sort of 'self-repairing' 'natural' wire that conducts the mana into the central 'engine'. Overtime, the denizens of the realms have mined and harvested the materials, which has resulted in a higher concentration in the center of the realms, where the leylines converge and feed into the 'engine'.
The souls of the Slivers, along with the various modifications they made to their biology, were designed to use the leylines to feed into the 'engine'. The souls of everything else, was not. The dead have their souls shredded, mere remnants at best remain, while the rest travels along the leylines. The edges, emotions, memories, non-sliver form/identity, all snag along the leylines, forming and growing crystals of mana along them. Unlike the veins/wires of metal that grow from the outside inwards, these crystals grow from the inside outwards.Due to the constant invasions of Eldrazi and the hyper territorial Slivers, major settlements have never appeared on any of the realms. There are small pockets of society on many of the realms, but that is just about all there is in nations.
Every realm has independently developed their own forms of sentient life, each though, is an odd mirror of one another, perhaps due to the innate interconnectivity each of the realms possess through the 'engine's connected planar portals and eternity columns. Every realm has beastial animalfolk known simply as the 'Kin', Aetherborn born from the quelling of the blind eternities, and Splinters individualists descended from the Sliver Hives. Some realms have Echoes, gestalt souls of the dead and/or Remnants, machines left by the Fomori.Oddly, Eldrazi incursions have become less frequent in the past decade or so. Similarly, Sliver attacks against Remnants travelling through the Hivelands have become more frequent.Despite each realm being objectively flat, a half-sphere of land and atmosphere, there is a visual illusion, perhaps induced by magic, perhaps some sort of gravitational distortion, that causes the realms to visually curve at roughly a rate of 0.009 degrees per kilometer to those on them.(For the purposes of anyone who looks into patterns, each integer is capable of being multiplied by 10 kilometers, starting where the prior ring ended and being that many km thick.)
(s is the number of legendary slivers in WUBRG there are, x is the number of legendary slivers in the color/hive, n is the number of unique arts for the legendary slivers in WUBRG (minus 1 for legion (crayon) and plus 1 for queen (duelmasters, L is the number of locus there are)))Each of the realms lack traditional days and nights, unlike many planes and planets, they are perpetually in a day, the light of the Exosphere causes a near shadowless perpetual dawn. An alien lighting to you or I.
On the horizon, beyond the skies, through the first two layers of the Exospheres lies a basic translation of the blind eternities into something real and definable. Shadows dance across the skies through this translation, some quick, others languid, but all are the forms of Eldrazi within the Chaos Wall.Often, these shadows will move into the exosphere and result in an eldrazi drone or two. This occurs frequently enough that the realmic denizens are unaware of the fact that Eldrazi are not normal forms of fauna.The rest of the non-sapient life are descendants of worms, faeries, insects, germs, gremlins, pests, and fungi in some manner.The center of each realm, save Corona, lies the engine surrounded by a sliver hive and the territory that hive has claimed. This territory is patrolled consistently enough by slivers that it is near impossible for any non-sliver faunal life to survive there, as they would be swarmed expeditiously by the hive. Remnants and Splinters are the only ones brave enough to risk entering the territories, as well as the occasional Dreamer military expedition.The Hivelands are large, usually preventing direct travel from one end of the realm to the other, instead, having to go around them, a journey that can take quite some time.The band around the hivelands, sandwiched between it and the Exosphere, is the area where the rest of the realm's matter exists. It is not rigorously patrolled by the local hive, but they do venture out into the areas occasionally.The edges of these lands are often consumed by Eldrazi or are desolate.When making it to the absolute edge of the realm, it simply drops off into the exosphere. The edge glowing with a silvery light as matter accumulates.The Exosphere is composed of three distinct spheres.The first sphere, the Glimmervoid, like a fog of silvery dust, translates mana into matter and accumulates it into the realm. Stone on stone, air on air, water on water, etc. It also serves to recycle matter that enters into it as material deposited into the realms.The second sphere, the Mana Confluence, is nothing but light, heat, and mana. This sphere translates the aether from the blind eternities into mana and energy. It slowly becomes converted into the realm's colors and serves as the generalized light source of each realm.The third and final sphere, the Echoes of Eternity, a shadowy scape that can drive those who look upon it mad. Shadows of the true forms of eldrazi move within, glimpses of the exosphere and multiverse within, and the destructive aether that annihilates matter infusing it all. This sphere somehow translating the blind eternities of the chaos wall into reality.The more colors of mana that needs to be processed or manufactured, the less stable the process is and the exponentially larger the exosphere needs to be.The bottom of the multiverse lies a single realm, that of no color. km total (exosphere included)
Komas - A realm devoid of colors of mana, unique for its mechanical Slivers. The first strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities possess only a single color of mana. km total (exosphere included)
Lumos - A realm of white mana
Souse - A realm of blue mana
Nocturne - A realm of black mana
Ignis - A realm of red mana
Sett - A realm of green mana The second strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities possess a pair of colors of mana. km total (exosphere included)
Unnamed 1 - A realm of wu mana
Ioxis - A realm of wb mana
Unnamed 2 - A realm of wr mana
Iethol - A realm of wg mana
Noxou - A realm of ub mana
Zyxkal - A realm of ur mana
Unnamed 3 - A realm of ug mana
Montresor - A realm of br mana
Unnamed 4 - A realm of bg mana
Unnamed 5 - A realm of rg mana The third strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities possess three colors of mana. km total (exosphere included)
Hytropo - A realm of wub
Unnamed 6 - A realm of wur
Unnamed 7 - A realm of wug
Unnamed 8 - A realm of wbr
Unnamed 9 - A realm of wbg
Unnamed 10 - A realm of wrg
Unnamed 11 - A realm of ubr
Unnamed 12 - A realm of ubg
Unnamed 13 - A realm of urg
Kithestra - A realm of brg The fourth strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities lack a single color of mana. km total (exosphere included)
Unnamed 14 - A realm of wubr
Unnamed 15 - A realm of wubg
Unnamed 16 - A realm of wurg
Unnamed 17 - A realm of wbrg
Unnamed 18 - A realm of ubrg The top of the multiverse lies a singular realm who's identity is all encompassing. km total diameter (exosphere included)
Corona - A realm of wubrg

info_showCustomIcons.description: Show custom icons for files and folders

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
